question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot Actuator /actuator/scheduledtasks or /actuator/beans endpoint to discover the retentionProperties bean configuration and identify the exact retention period, then time a fee transaction to fall just outside the retention window before triggering a premature prune, causing the fee record to be deleted while the corresponding transaction record remains?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTableName(RetentionRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getTableName() uses getInterfaces()[0] without bounds checking, and if a RetentionRepository proxy class has no interfaces (e.g., due to a CGLIB proxy), this throws an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException that is caught by the outer catch block, silently skipping the pruning of that fee repository and leaving stale fee records that should have been pruned?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the counters TreeMap (line 102) which is shared across all prune() iterations, causing the cumulative count to grow unboundedly across iterations and overflow Long.MAX_VALUE, producing negative counter values in the final log message that mask the true scale of assessed_custom_fee record deletion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the toInstant(long nanos) method (line 95-97) which calls Instant.ofEpochSecond(0L, nanos) with a nanos value derived from consensusEnd, and if consensusEnd is a very large long value, this throws a DateTimeException that propagates up through the log.info() call and is caught by the outer catch block, halting the prune cycle after some assessed_custom_fee records have already been deleted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that retentionProperties is a Spring @Component bean with @Data (Lombok-generated setters), and if any configuration refresh mechanism (e.g., Spring Cloud Config) is accessible, they can push a configuration update that sets period to a negative value, causing the next prune() execution to delete all assessed_custom_fee records regardless of age?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFileIterator.hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the findNextBetween(endTimestamp, max.getConsensusEnd()) call (line 136) where endTimestamp is computed as current.getConsensusEnd() + batchPeriod, and if batchPeriod is negative (due to a negative Duration), endTimestamp could be less than current.getConsensusEnd(), causing the iterator to move backwards and re-prune already-processed assessed_custom_fee records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shouldPrune(table) logic in RetentionProperties where 'include.isEmpty() || include.contains(table)' means an empty include set prunes ALL tables, and by ensuring the include set is empty (default) while manipulating the period, cause all fee-related tables including assessed_custom_fee and custom_fee_history to be pruned simultaneously outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() method logs the retentionPeriod and maxTimestamp (line 52-53) before pruning begins, and by monitoring these log outputs through an exposed logging endpoint, determine the exact timestamp boundary and craft fee transactions that fall just within the prune window to ensure their fee records are deleted while the transaction itself remains?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the transactionOperations.executeWithoutResult() wrapping (line 73) which executes all repository prune operations in a single database transaction, and by causing a deadlock on the assessed_custom_fee table from another connection, force the transaction to roll back after custom_fee_history has been pruned but before assessed_custom_fee is pruned, creating an inconsistent fee state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Scheduled annotation's fixedDelayString which evaluates retentionProperties.getFrequency().toMillis() at startup, and if the frequency is set to 1 millisecond via configuration injection, cause the prune() job to execute thousands of times per second, each time potentially deleting assessed_custom_fee records and overwhelming the database with fee record deletions outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFileIterator.hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition check 'if (next.isEmpty()) { current = null; return false; }' (lines 137-139) where findNextBetween() returns empty due to a gap in RecordFile timestamps, causing the iterator to terminate early and skip pruning of assessed_custom_fee records in the gap period, leaving stale fee records that should have been pruned per the retention policy?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that retentionProperties.getPeriod().toNanos() is used as the offset for findLatestWithOffset(), and since Duration.toNanos() can return Long.MIN_VALUE for very large negative durations, the SQL query 'consensus_end < (max) - Long.MIN_VALUE' could cause integer overflow in the database, returning unexpected RecordFiles and causing assessed_custom_fee records to be pruned outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(RecordFileIterator)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that repository.prune(endTimestamp) returns an int (not long) in the RetentionRepository interface, and if more than Integer.MAX_VALUE assessed_custom_fee records are pruned in a single call, the return value overflows to a negative number, causing counters.merge() to subtract from the counter and produce incorrect pruning statistics that mask the true scale of fee record deletion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot property binding for 'hiero.mirror.importer.retention.exclude' to inject a set containing all non-fee tables (e.g., 'record_file', 'transaction', 'crypto_transfer'), causing the prune job to exclusively delete assessed_custom_fee and custom_fee_history records while preserving all other data, selectively erasing fee evidence outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: RecordFileIterator.hasNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the initialization path where 'current == null' triggers findNextBetween(0, max.getConsensusEnd()) (line 111), and by manipulating the database to have a RecordFile with consensusEnd=1 (near timestamp 0), cause the iterator to start pruning from the very beginning of the ledger history, deleting assessed_custom_fee records from the genesis block outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() method catches all Exception types (line 61) and only logs them, meaning that even if a security exception or access control violation occurs during fee record pruning, the job continues silently, allowing partial fee record deletion to proceed without alerting operators?
